seoTitle: How to Add Handwriting to a Google Doc (2026)
metaDescription: Add a handwritten signature or freehand note to Google Docs using the built-in drawing tool's scribble feature, no external app required.
intro: Adding a personal signature or handwritten annotation to a Google Doc doesn't require leaving the app. This guide shows how to use the built-in drawing canvas and its scribble tool to write directly with a mouse, trackpad, or stylus.

steps.heading: Open the drawing tool
steps.body: Click Insert, hover over Drawing, and select New to launch the drawing canvas inside Google Docs.

steps.heading: Select the Scribble line option
steps.body: In the drawing toolbar, click the line dropdown and choose Scribble from the bottom of the list to enable freehand writing.

steps.heading: Write your handwriting
steps.body: Click and drag with your mouse, trackpad, or stylus to write your signature or note directly on the canvas.

steps.heading: Adjust stroke color and weight
steps.body: Use the border color and line weight tools next to the scribble option to fine-tune how your handwriting looks.

steps.heading: Save and place it in your document
steps.body: Click 'Save and Close' to insert the drawing, then adjust text wrapping so it sits neatly alongside your existing content.

faq.question: Can I edit my handwriting after inserting it?
faq.answer: Yes, double-click the drawing to reopen the canvas and make changes at any time.

faq.question: Does this work with a touchscreen or stylus?
faq.answer: Yes, a stylus or touchscreen gives more precise control than a mouse for natural-looking handwriting.

faq.question: Can I use handwriting as a signature on contracts?
faq.answer: It works for informal use, but for legally binding signatures you may want a dedicated e-signature tool.

faq.question: Will the drawing resize if I change the page layout?
faq.answer: The drawing keeps its set dimensions, so you may need to manually resize it after major layout changes.
